{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),

        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),","new":"            nn.Linear(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, FC_HIDDEN),","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The model is still shallow and lacks any spatial downsampling. Adding a second stage that halves resolution and doubles channels should give the residual net a more useful hierarchy for CIFAR-10 while keeping the rest of the training setup unchanged."}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["caching"],"rationale":"With the hierarchical residual net in place, the next cheap gain is to expose more data per fixed step and soften overconfident targets. A larger batch and mild label smoothing are low-risk adjustments that often help CIFAR-10 validation loss without changing the model structure."}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The model is now stronger and the batch size increased, so the most likely remaining gain is a larger optimization step. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current model is already fairly strong, so the next likely gain is to relax regularization a bit rather than add more capacity. Lowering weight decay should let the network fit the fixed-step budget more fully without changing the training loop."}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),

            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),

        )","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(

            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, channels * CHANNEL_MULT * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The network is now deep enough that the remaining error is more likely to be regularization mismatch than missing capacity. Reducing label smoothing slightly keeps the training recipe intact while letting the model fit the validation objective more closely."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The model is now expressive enough that extra target smoothing may be suppressing the exact loss the checker measures. Removing label smoothing aligns the training objective with the validation objective and is the smallest high-signal regularization change left."}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The last no-smoothing change slightly hurt validation loss, so the most informative next move is to restore the regularization setting that previously produced the best visible score. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The best regularization setting is back in place, so the remaining high-signal move is to soften the optimizer step size a bit. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The prior run at `2e-3` was the best visible setting, and the lower rate degraded validation loss. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The current optimum seems to sit near the AdamW learning-rate scale rather than the regularization settings. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The 2e-3 setting remains the best observed learning-rate scale, while 2.25e-3 regressed. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current three-stage model is already strong, so the remaining gain is more likely to come from easing regularization than changing structure again. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The model has already benefited from progressively weaker regularization, and the remaining decay is tiny.
